# Grocery List

- 14 oz Thai stir fry noodles
- 2 limes
- 2 tablespoons tamari (or regular soy sauce)
- 1/4 cup sweet and sour sauce
- 2 bell peppers
- 4 garlic cloves
- 1 package extra-firm tofu
- 1/4 cup roasted peanuts
- 2 eggs
- 2 green onions
- 1 large poblano
- 2 roma tomatoes
- 1 can of black beans
- 1 tablespoon southwest spice
- 6-8 flour tortillas
- 1 jar of salsa verde
- 4 oz monterey jack cheese
- 2 tablespoons sour cream
- 1 yellow onion
- 4 cloves of garlic
- 1 red pepper
- 1 14.5oz can crushed fire-roasted tomatoes
- 1/4 oz cilantro
- 4 eggs
- feta cheese
- pitas or naan
- 1 cup spinach
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 1 banana
- 4-5 mint leaves
- 1 cup frozen mango
- 1 cup frozen pineapple


\newpage

## Dinner Recipe 1


![Pad Thai](website/images/PadThai.jpg){width=50%}


- 14 oz Thai stir fry noodles
- 2 limes
- 2 tablespoons tamari (or regular soy sauce)
- 1/4 cup sweet and sour sauce
- 2 bell peppers
- 4 garlic cloves
- 1 package extra-firm tofu
- 1/4 cup roasted peanuts
- 2 eggs



1. Boil Thai noodles according to package instructions. Rince under cold water and coat with oil.
1. Drain tofu, cut into bite-sized pieces, and season with salt and pepper.
1. Thinly slice peppers, mince garlic, chop peanuts.
1. Squeeze 2 tablespoons lime juice into a small bowl. Mix in tamari and sweet and sour sauce.
1. Heat oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Cook tofu until golden brown (5-10 minutes). Move to a paper towel lined plate.
1. In the same pan, add oil and cook bell peppers until tender. Add to the plate with the tofu.
1. In the same pan, add oil and garlic. Cook 1-2 minutes until browned.
1. Add noodles and sauce and cook about 3-5 minutes, until noodles absorb some sauce.
1. Move noodles to one side of the pan. Add a little oil and two eggs. Scramble the eggs until almost set, then stir them into the noodles.
1. Mix in tofu, peppers, and peanuts.
1. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve with lime wedges.



\newpage


## Dinner Recipe 2


![Enchiladas](website/images/Enchiladas.jpg){width=50%}


- 2 green onions
- 1 large poblano
- 2 roma tomatoes
- 1 can of black beans
- 1 tablespoon southwest spice
- 6-8 flour tortillas
- 1 jar of salsa verde
- 4 oz monterey jack cheese
- 2 tablespoons sour cream



1. Dice tomato and poblano. Thinly slice green onions, separating greens and whites. Drain (save liquid) and rinse black beans.
1. Cook poblano in oil over medium heat until softened (5-7 minutes).
1. Add almost all of the southwest seasoning (except a half tsp), tomato, green onion whites, half the black beans, and 1/8 cup black bean liquid. Cook another 5-10 minutes, until peppers are tender.
1. Add remanining beans and a drizzle of oil in a small sauce pan over medium heat.
1. Add 1/4 cup black bean liquid and bring to a simmer. 6. Add 1 tablespoon of butter and cook until melted.
1. Using a silicone masher, the black beans and butter until smooth.
1. Spread black bean mixture over tortillas and evenly split filling between tortillas. Roll up tortillas and place them in a 9 x 13 baking dish.
1. Top with salsa verde and monterey jack cheese. Bake at 450 for 5-10 minutes until cheese melts and starts to brown.
1. Combine sour cream and 1/2 tsp southwest seasoning. Mix in 1 teaspoon of water at a time until sauce can be drizzled. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
1. Serve enchiladas with sauce and green onion greens.



\newpage


## Dinner Recipe 3


![Shakshuka](website/images/Shakshuka.jpg){width=50%}


- 1 yellow onion
- 4 cloves of garlic
- 1 red pepper
- 1 14.5oz can crushed fire-roasted tomatoes
- 1/4 oz cilantro
- 4 eggs
- feta cheese
- pitas or naan



1. Chop onion and red pepper. Mince garlic.
1. In a frying pan, heat one tablespoon of oil over medium heat and cook onions for about five minutes.
1. Add crushed tomatoes and cook until boiling. Reduce heat and simmer another 5-10 minutes, until the sauce is thickened.
1. Using the back of a spoon, make four indentations in the sauce. Crack an egg in each indentation. Cook seven minutes longer until eggs are just set.
1. Remove from heat, and sprinkle feta and cilantro.
1. Serve in a bowl with toasted pitas or naan.



\newpage


## Breakfast Recipe


![Tropical Smoothie](website/images/TropicalSmoothie.jpg){width=50%}


- 1 cup spinach
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 1 banana
- 4-5 mint leaves
- 1 cup frozen mango
- 1 cup frozen pineapple



1. In a blender, combine 1 cup of water, spinach, ginger, banana, and mint until smooth.
1. Add mango and pineapple, and blend until smooth.
1. Serve in mason jars!



\newpage
